query_normalized: llmxive follow-up: extending "does vla even know the basics? measuring commonsense and world knowled" computer science
rationale: This paper addresses the user's question by providing empirical evidence on the specific mechanism of "world knowledge" limitations in Large Vision-Language Models (LVLMs) and offering a canonical solution (internet-augmented generation) to measure and improve this capability, satisfying criteria (a) for same-mechanism evidence and (b) for measuring the dependent variable (knowledge accuracy) on the relevant domain.
